Trusted by thousands of teams
Built for Pairing
Engineers deserve sharp tools
Generic screen sharing tools just won't cut it.
Tuple is purpose-built
    for an exceptional pairing experience.
Ridiculously crisp audio and video
Tuple streams in super high resolution (up to 5K) so you can read your pair's tiny programming
      font.
End-to-end encryption
All audio, video, and screen share data is end-to-end encrypted, and is never sent to Tuple's
      servers. We take your privacy extremely seriously.
Seamless collaboration
Snappy remote control combined with clear audio and video make you feel like you're sitting
      right next to your pair.

One click to swap who’s sharing
It just takes one click to switch who's sharing when you're on a call. Easily go back and
        forth with your pair on a tough problem.
Testimonials
Don't take our word for it
Here are some of the nice things people have said about Tuple.
Tuple has become
our default tool for remote pairing at Shopify
. The teams love it.
Farhan Thawar
VP of Engineering, Shopify
It's the best tool for remote pair programming.
Latency is important, keyboard binding all just works. It feels like you're working on a shared
      computer.
Andrés Taylor
Developer, PlanetScale
So impressed with Tuple. It makes
pair programming remotely with co-workers and friends fun again
.
Michael Feldstein
Staff Engineer, Figma
Tuple allows me to thrive as an independent developer
, easily chatting with freelance clients, mentoring others, and collaborating on open-source
      projects.
Mikaela Caron
Independent iOS Engineer
Tuple spread like wildfire throughout the entire Clerk team, and quickly became an
integral part of our internal communications
.
Braden Sidoti
Co-founder & CTO, Clerk
Small footprint
Respectful of system resources and screen real estate
Native where it counts
The core of Tuple is a natively compiled, cross-platform C++ engine. It's not just another Electron app.
Obsessed with performance
We've been hacking on Tuple for years to make it as fast and efficient as possible. We're stingy with your CPU cycles.
Runs in your menu bar
Do you hate a cluttered dock or task bar? Well, us too. Tuple stays out of your way until you need it.
Designed for privacy
Fearless
screen
sharing with
App Veil
Your teammates only see what you want them to see, nothing more.
Fearless
screen
sharing with App Veil
Hide sensitive apps and notifications, and share your desktop with confidence.
Workflow
Built for developers, by developers
We're programmers too. We built Tuple to fit the way engineers work.

Tools for programmers should be programmable.
Triggers let you hook into Tuple's call
        events and run arbitrary code.

Keyboard shortcuts for everything
Keep your hands on the keyboard and stay in flow with fully customizable shortcuts.
For macOS and Windows
It doesn't matter whether your team is using macOS or Windows — Tuple works great on both.
Effective and fun
Liven up your pairing sessions
Build cool stuff with your teammates – and have a good time in the process.
Screen annotations
Draw or type on a shared screen to draw attention to something or emphasize a point. Or use
        Tuple as a shared virtual whiteboard.
Emoji reactions
Show the rest of team how you're feeling with on-screen emoji reactions.
Animations
Finally figure out that gnarly race condition? Drop a giant "ship it" animation into the
        pairing session.
❤️

Pricing
Tuple pays for itself in happier, more productive engineers
Get started with a
14-day free trial
.
Engineering teams
$30
/
per user
per month
Unlimited pairing sessions
Pair with external collaborators for free
To pair with Tuple, only one of the users in the session must be a paid
                          user.
Learn more
Single sign-on (SSO)
Get started for free
Enterprises
Custom pricing available
Contact us for more information
Everything on the teams plan
Custom contracts
Payment via invoice/PO
Reduced per-user pricing at scale
Contact sales
No love, no pay.
If you're unhappy with
          Tuple at any point, we'll refund your most recent monthly payment.
90% off for a year for startups
If your company is less than two years old and has fewer than 50 employees, we'll add
              a one-year 90% discount to your subscription.
Apply for discount
Tuple is free for OSS
If you do significant OSS work, we'll donate free Tuple licenses to collaborate with
              contributors/maintainers.
Get Tuple for free
Testimonials
Developers talk about Tuple best
Here are some of the nice things folks have said about us.
Tuple is probably the highest rated tool we've implemented. The developers who use it
can't imagine working remote without it
.
Nick Cash
VP of Engineering, Jam City
I've been in meetings with 10 people on Tuple, cycling through who's driving, and
it's been incredibly productive
. If we had to try to do that on another platform, it wouldn't be worth it.
Cassidy Scheffer
Staff Software Developer, Wealthsimple
Tuple is integral for us as a remote team.
It creates the illusion that there is nothing in the way. It’s faster, easier and more
            seamless than anything else we’ve tried.
Ian Lesperance
Senior Principal Software Engineer, Vimeo
FAQ
Frequently asked questions
Here's what you need to know about your Tuple license, based on the questions we get asked
          the most.
What platforms do you support?
Tuple is available for macOS and Windows.
Does it cost anything to pair with people outside my organization?
Nope! Paid users (the default) can pair with external collaborators (clients,
            consultants, interviewees) for free. Learn more in
our docs
.
How do you handle security and privacy?
Carefully! Check out our
security details
and
privacy policy
.
Can I get a refund?
We're not in the business of making money from unhappy users. If you're unhappy with
            Tuple at any point, we'll refund your most recent monthly payment. See our
refund policy
for more details.
Tuple

Changelog
What's new in Tuple
The latest updates and improvements for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
View full changelog
macOS
macOS 3.0.0
Added
Upscale Content:
we've added a local, per-viewer toggle that sharpens incoming screen share content.
Redesigned notifications
: cards anchor to the menu bar status item, stack instead of replacing each other, and dissolve when dismissed.
For mute notifications, you can now choose to dismiss, snooze them for a call, or never show them again.
Fixed
Notifications no longer appear in your outgoing video stream during a screen share, and remote participants can't click on them
Visual artifacts in screen share at certain resolutions and with some fonts
Webcam grid window now appears when you turn on your webcam (or click "Detach Preview") while waiting for a closed call link to start
Guest-to-host pinch-to-zoom magnify gesture when someone on a Mac is hosting.
Ghost cursors left behind when a participant leaves a screen share they were already in before the share window opened.
Crash and stuck cursors during peer reconnection or screen-driver changes on macOS Tahoe.
Notifications and the incoming-call window now position correctly under Universal Control and on Macs with a notch, instead of falling back to screen center.
macOS
macOS 2.4.3
Added
Speaking-while-muted notification — get a heads-up with an "Unmute Me" action when you start talking while muted
Tuple now lets you know if your audio output device is muted during a call
"Mute Audio" action in the launcher, grid, and toolbar participant menus
"Ask to Unmute" action in the screen-sharing window and webcam window participant menus
"Ask to Share Webcam", "Ask to Unmute", and "Mute Audio" are now available in the compact webcam view participant dropdown
Changed
Lowered the screen share minimum bitrate floor so screen share can throttle further under congestion without starving audio
Webcam window in conversation mode redesigned for smoother layout and animations
Fixed
Incorrect layout in the conversation mode when joining a call with participants sharing their webcams
Spurious empty annotation when the emoji picker was open
High CPU usage when viewing the screen share window
macOS
macOS 2.4.2
Added
Sending GIFs
: you can now send animated GIFs via the reactions menu when viewing a shared screen. Because why not?
Alternate menu bar icon
: choose between the Tuple logo and our cute lil' "Pear Buddy"
Pick-window mode
: in the partial screen share chooser, you can now set the screen share boundaries to match the dimensions of a specific window. Note that this just sets the screen share boundaries - if the window moves or gets hidden, the boundary won't change.
Emoji picker support for text annotations
Caller's email is now shown in incoming call notifications
Changed
Zoom modifier changed from Command to Option, so it no longer clashes with Cmd+V paste or Ctrl+Cmd+Space emoji insertion
Faster reconnection after a network drop
Toolbar is now more visible in light mode
Linux
Linux 2026.04.08.0
Added
Support for annotations in Wayland
: the annotation overlay now renders natively in Wayland! Be sure to set
XDG_SESSION_TYPE=wayland
to enable this functionality.
New CLI commands
: we've added
call
(to call a favorite contact),
ls
(to list contacts), and
favorite $USER_ID
(to favorite a contact). See more in our docs.
App icon
: Tuple now has an icon 😅 This should make it way easier to find Tuple in your task bar / app switcher
Email address shown in incoming call window
Error message when screen capture fails
X11 vsync support
Changed
Incoming call window has been redesigned
macOS
macOS 2.4.1
Fixed
Crash that could happen after logging out
Crash on the Sound Check window
Tuple

analysis.name: Tuple
analysis.tagline: The best remote pair programming app on macOS and Windows
analysis.value_proposition: Purpose-built pair programming tool with razor-sharp screen sharing, crisp audio, and seamless remote control so distributed engineers can code together as if sitting side-by-side.
analysis.ideal_customer: Software engineering teams at mid-market to enterprise companies doing regular remote pair programming, especially on macOS

features.name: high-resolution screen sharing
features.description: Streams screen share at up to 5K resolution so code in small fonts is readable
features.evidence_quote: Tuple streams in super high resolution (up to 5K) so you can read your pair's tiny programming font.

features.name: end-to-end encryption
features.description: All audio, video, and screen share data is E2E encrypted and never transits Tuple's servers
features.evidence_quote: All audio, video, and screen share data is end-to-end encrypted, and is never sent to Tuple's servers.

features.name: remote control
features.description: One participant can control the other's machine during a pairing session
features.evidence_quote: Snappy remote control combined with clear audio and video make you feel like you're sitting right next to your pair.

features.name: one-click screen swap
features.description: Switch who is sharing their screen with a single click during a call
features.evidence_quote: It just takes one click to switch who's sharing when you're on a call.

features.name: app veil
features.description: Hide sensitive apps and notifications from your pair before sharing your desktop
features.evidence_quote: Hide sensitive apps and notifications, and share your desktop with confidence.

features.name: native c++ engine
features.description: Core is natively compiled cross-platform C++, not an Electron wrapper, for performance and efficiency
features.evidence_quote: The core of Tuple is a natively compiled, cross-platform C++ engine. It's not just another Electron app.

features.name: menu bar app
features.description: Runs unobtrusively in the menu bar rather than the dock to minimize visual clutter
features.evidence_quote: Tuple stays out of your way until you need it.

features.name: triggers
features.description: Run custom scripts/code in response to Tuple call events (call start, participant join/leave, call end)
features.evidence_quote: Run your own code in response to Tuple events

features.name: api
features.description: Programmatic API to integrate Tuple events into external workflows
features.evidence_quote: API — Run your own code in response to Tuple events

features.name: slack integration
features.description: Start Tuple calls directly from Slack
features.evidence_quote: Slack — Start Tuple calls right from Slack

features.name: google calendar integration
features.description: Add Tuple call links to Google Calendar events
features.evidence_quote: Google Calendar — Add a Tuple call link to your calendar events

features.name: apple calendar integration
features.description: Add Tuple call links to Apple Calendar events
features.evidence_quote: Apple Calendar — Same as above, but for Apple Calendar

features.name: linux support
features.description: Alpha Linux client available with screen share, annotations, and CLI commands
features.evidence_quote: Pear — Tuple for Linux alpha is available!

features.name: sso
features.description: Single sign-on authentication for teams
features.evidence_quote: Single sign-on (SSO)

features.name: external collaborator pairing
features.description: Paid users can pair with external guests (clients, contractors, interviewees) at no extra charge
features.evidence_quote: Pair with external collaborators for free — only one of the users in the session must be a paid user.

features.name: annotations
features.description: Draw or annotate on the shared screen during a session; includes emoji picker support
features.evidence_quote: Support for annotations in Wayland: the annotation overlay now renders natively in Wayland!

features.name: gif reactions
features.description: Send animated GIFs via the reactions menu while viewing a shared screen
features.evidence_quote: Sending GIFs: you can now send animated GIFs via the reactions menu when viewing a shared screen.

features.name: webcam support
features.description: Share webcam video during pairing sessions with a dedicated webcam grid window
features.evidence_quote: Webcam grid window now appears when you turn on your webcam

features.name: speaking-while-muted notification
features.description: Detects when you speak while muted and surfaces an Unmute Me action
features.evidence_quote: Speaking-while-muted notification — get a heads-up with an 'Unmute Me' action when you start talking while muted

features.name: ask to unmute
features.description: Request a participant to unmute themselves from the host controls
features.evidence_quote: 'Ask to Unmute' action in the screen-sharing window and webcam window participant menus

features.name: ask to share webcam
features.description: Prompt a participant to turn on their webcam during a session
features.evidence_quote: 'Ask to Share Webcam', 'Ask to Unmute', and 'Mute Audio' are now available in the compact webcam view participant dropdown

features.name: partial screen share / pick-window mode
features.description: Set screen share boundaries to match a specific window's dimensions for partial desktop sharing
features.evidence_quote: Pick-window mode: in the partial screen share chooser, you can now set the screen share boundaries to match the dimensions of a specific window.

features.name: upscale content
features.description: Per-viewer local toggle that sharpens incoming screen share content using upscaling
features.evidence_quote: Upscale Content: we've added a local, per-viewer toggle that sharpens incoming screen share content.

features.name: rooms
features.description: Persistent rooms for pairing sessions (referenced in docs navigation)
features.evidence_quote: Pairing with Tuple — Learn how to share screens, use rooms, pair with guests, and navigate calls.

features.name: linux cli
features.description: Command-line interface for Linux to call contacts, list contacts, and manage favorites
features.evidence_quote: New CLI commands: we've added call (to call a favorite contact), ls (to list contacts), and favorite $USER_ID

features.name: usage reporting
features.description: Team-level usage reports for admins
features.evidence_quote: Reporting

features.name: team management
features.description: Manage invites, roles, and member permissions for the team
features.evidence_quote: Team Management — Manage invites, billing, SSO, team roles, and usage reports.

features.name: billing management
features.description: Self-serve subscription and billing management including invoices and payment
features.evidence_quote: Billing and Subscription

features.name: alternate menu bar icon
features.description: Choose between the Tuple logo and a 'Pear Buddy' icon in the menu bar
features.evidence_quote: Alternate menu bar icon: choose between the Tuple logo and our cute lil' 'Pear Buddy'

features.name: pinch-to-zoom magnify
features.description: Guest-to-host pinch-to-zoom gesture to magnify content on the host's screen share
features.evidence_quote: Guest-to-host pinch-to-zoom magnify gesture when someone on a Mac is hosting.

features.name: mute audio control
features.description: Mute participants from the launcher, grid, toolbar, and compact webcam view
features.evidence_quote: 'Mute Audio' action in the launcher, grid, and toolbar participant menus

features.name: startup discount program
features.description: 90% off for one year for companies under 2 years old with fewer than 50 employees
features.evidence_quote: 90% off for a year for startups — less than two years old and fewer than 50 employees

features.name: oss free license
features.description: Free Tuple licenses donated to significant open-source contributors and maintainers
features.evidence_quote: Tuple is free for OSS — If you do significant OSS work, we'll donate free Tuple licenses.

differentiators: Native C++ engine — explicitly not Electron, purpose-built for performance
End-to-end encrypted with no data transiting Tuple's servers
External collaborators can join for free — only one paid seat required per session
App Veil to hide sensitive content before sharing desktop
Purpose-built for pair programming, not a generic screen sharing tool
Linux alpha support in addition to macOS and Windows

recent_ships.what: Upscale Content: local per-viewer toggle to sharpen incoming screen share; redesigned notifications with stacking and snooze
recent_ships.source: macOS 3.0.0 — Upscale Content: we've added a local, per-viewer toggle that sharpens incoming screen share content.

recent_ships.what: Speaking-while-muted notification with Unmute Me action; Ask to Unmute and Ask to Share Webcam controls
recent_ships.source: macOS 2.4.3 — Speaking-while-muted notification — get a heads-up with an 'Unmute Me' action

recent_ships.what: GIF reactions in screen share view; pick-window mode for partial screen share; emoji picker for annotations; Pear Buddy alternate icon
recent_ships.source: macOS 2.4.2 — Sending GIFs: you can now send animated GIFs via the reactions menu when viewing a shared screen.

recent_ships.what: Linux: Wayland annotation support; new CLI commands (call, ls, favorite); app icon; redesigned incoming call window
recent_ships.source: Linux 2026.04.08.0 — Support for annotations in Wayland: the annotation overlay now renders natively in Wayland!

praised: Tuple has become our default tool for remote pairing at Shopify. The teams love it. — Farhan Thawar, VP Engineering, Shopify
It's the best tool for remote pair programming. Latency is important, keyboard binding all just works. It feels like you're working on a shared computer. — Andrés Taylor, PlanetScale
Tuple is probably the highest rated tool we've implemented. The developers who use it can't imagine working remote without it. — Nick Cash, VP Engineering, Jam City
I've been in meetings with 10 people on Tuple, cycling through who's driving, and it's been incredibly productive. — Cassidy Scheffer, Wealthsimple
It creates the illusion that there is nothing in the way. It's faster, easier and more seamless than anything else we've tried. — Ian Lesperance, Vimeo
Makes pair programming remotely with co-workers and friends fun again. — Michael Feldstein, Figma
Tuple allows me to thrive as an independent developer, easily chatting with freelance clients, mentoring others. — Mikaela Caron, iOS Engineer
Tuple spread like wildfire throughout the entire Clerk team. — Braden Sidoti, CTO, Clerk
HN thread received 276 upvotes with 144 comments at launch

tiers.name: Engineering Teams
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited pairing sessions, SSO, free external collaborator pairing, 14-day trial

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Everything in Teams plus custom contracts, invoice/PO payment, reduced per-user pricing at scale

pricing.notes: 14-day free trial on signup. 90% discount for 1 year available to startups under 2 years old with <50 employees. Free licenses for significant OSS contributors. Refund policy covers most recent monthly payment. External collaborators (guests) join sessions free — only the host needs a paid seat.
